question_text: A crowbar 1.2 m long is used to lift a rock. The pivot is placed 0.3 m from the end of the bar that touches the rock. If the rock exerts a downward force of 600 N, what minimum effort force (at the other end of the crowbar) is needed to lift it?
explanation: The load arm is 0.3 m and the effort arm is 1.2 − 0.3 = 0.9 m. Using the principle of moments: load moment = effort moment, so 600 N × 0.3 m = effort × 0.9 m. Solving: effort = (600 × 0.3) ÷ 0.9 = 150 N.
